name: music-pykg

metadata.name: music_pykg
metadata.summary: Python tools to read MUSIC output files

documentation:
# Python tooling for MUSIC

`music_pykg` offers a Python API to read MUSIC output files.

documentation: Utility to read profile1d dat files.

classes.name: music_pykg.prof1d.Prof1d

methods.name: music_pykg.prof1d.Prof1d.with_path_hint
documentation:
Find a profile file from the given hint.

Args:
    path_hint: either the path to the profile file, or the path to the
        folder containing the profile file.  In the latter case, the
        parser tries to find a file and fails if none is found or there
        is ambiguity.

Prof1d parser.

This parses 1D profiles data. Files are expected to be in the following
format (with arbitrary number of parameters and columns):

```txt
param_label1 param_label2
param_value1 param_value2
column1 column2 column3
val1_1  val1_2  val1_3
val2_1  val2_2  val2_3
val3_1  val3_2  val3_3
val4_1  val4_2  val4_3
val5_1  val5_2  val5_3
```

A mapping between parameter labels and values is accessible via the `params`
property. Profiles are accessible via the `profs` property.

If there are no parameters, set `params_present` to `False`.

methods.name: music_pykg.cpu_decomp.PrimeFactors.common_divisors
documentation:
Yield all divisors of `self` that are also divisors of `other`.

For instance, PrimeFactors.of(30).common_divisors(PrimeFactors.of(24))
yields 1, 2, 3, 6 (as PrimeFactors instances).

Evaluate the relative path for a given root.

Implementations should return the resolved relative path with the

classes.name: music_test.self_checks.ReportNorms
documentation:
Report norms of input dump object to log messages, always returning a successful status.

NOTE: the norms are computed pointwise naively, i.e. they are seen as norms on data arrays,
not as proper integral norms e.g. on the sphere.

classes.name: music_test.repo.TestRepository

methods.name: music_test.repo.TestRepository.path
documentation: Path to the directory of this repository

A repository of tests in the new format, relying on `test_config.py` files
for test discovery and configuration.

Tests are defined a subdirectories of `root_dir` that contain a `test_config.py` file.
A test specification file named `<root_dir>/some/test/test_config.py`
will define the test named `some/test`.

Each test specification file must be a valid Python script,
and is expected to define a variable `test` of the class `Test`,
which encapsulates all the required information for this test.
